an1.258:1.1: "This, *bhikkhus*, is the foremost among my female lay disciples who went for refuge first — namely, Sujātā, daughter of Seniya.
an1.259:1.1: … Among donors — namely, Visākhā, Migāra's mother.
an1.261:1.1: … Among those who dwell in *mettā* — namely, Sāmāvatī.
an1.262:1.1: … Among those who meditate — namely, Uttarā, Nanda's mother.
an1.263:1.1: … Among those who give fine things — namely, Suppavāsā, daughter of the Koliyans.
an1.264:1.1: … Among those who tend the sick — namely, the lay disciple Suppiyā.
an1.265:1.1: … Among those with unwavering confidence — namely, Kātiyānī.
an1.266:1.1: … Among those who are trusting — namely, Nakulamātā, the householder's wife.
an1.267:1.1: … Among those whose confidence comes from hearing — namely, Kāḷī, the lay disciple from Kuraraghara."
